segments.title: Peking Plan and Polish destroyer escape
segments.summary: The lecture frames the Peking Plan as a practical way to preserve Polish destroyers for Allied use before Germany could trap them in the Baltic.
segments.body: The discussion captures the episode's opening case study. Dr. Clarke explains that Polish planning sent surface ships toward Britain so they could support wider Allied operations instead of being lost in an exposed Baltic position. The destroyers received the signal before the invasion, passed through Danish waters, linked up with Royal Navy destroyers, and reached Leith on 1 September 1939.

evidence.note: The transcript introduces the Peking Plan and explains why Polish surface ships were to get away for use with Britain and Romanian supply routes.
The passage follows the signal, Baltic transit, Royal Navy contact, and arrival at Leith on 1 September 1939.
The closing assessment says the move was controversial but strategically sensible because the ships would be useful elsewhere.
